section_name: Use of flashing amber warning lights or nonflashing blue lights on tow car at scene of traffic hazard
text:
(1) A tow car which is equipped with flashing amber warning lights pursuant to NRS 484D.185 may display flashing amber warning lights to the front, sides or rear of the tow car when at the scene of a traffic hazard.

(2) A tow car which is equipped with lamps that emit nonflashing blue light pursuant to NRS 484D.475 may display nonflashing blue light to the rear of the tow car when at the scene of a traffic hazard.

(3) Any flashing amber warning light or lamps that emit nonflashing blue light used pursuant to this section must comply with the standards approved by the Department.
markdown:
## Title 43
### Chapter 484B
##### § 484B.748 — Use of flashing amber warning lights or nonflashing blue lights on tow car at scene of traffic hazard

(1) A tow car which is equipped with flashing amber warning lights pursuant to NRS 484D.185 may display flashing amber warning lights to the front, sides or rear of the tow car when at the scene of a traffic hazard.

(2) A tow car which is equipped with lamps that emit nonflashing blue light pursuant to NRS 484D.475 may display nonflashing blue light to the rear of the tow car when at the scene of a traffic hazard.

(3) Any flashing amber warning light or lamps that emit nonflashing blue light used pursuant to this section must comply with the standards approved by the Department.

subsections.text: A tow car which is equipped with flashing amber warning lights pursuant to NRS 484D.185 may display flashing amber warning lights to the front, sides or rear of the tow car when at the scene of a traffic hazard.
A tow car which is equipped with lamps that emit nonflashing blue light pursuant to NRS 484D.475 may display nonflashing blue light to the rear of the tow car when at the scene of a traffic hazard.
Any flashing amber warning light or lamps that emit nonflashing blue light used pursuant to this section must comply with the standards approved by the Department.
